text: The Alto Lakes Special Zoning District Commission will hold a Regular meeting on Thursday 18 June 2020 at
9:00am at the Alto Lakes Golf and Country Club, 1 Mulligan Drive, Alto, NM. Actual meeting room to be posted
before the meeting. Agenda to be posted on ALSZD.org no later than seventy-two hours prior to meeting.
Motion: Nicki Second: Roma 4. Approval of Meeting Agenda:
Motion: Roma Second: Nicki Approval of Meeting Minutes for: 5.
REQUEST: Replace existing rear deck (& removal of old 180 sq. ft. Black metal)
REQUEST: Complete rebuild of existing deck.
REQUEST: Small roof inside of existing courtyard in back of home
Comments: Owner conveyed to Bryan he had submitted Joint Permit in March; not
present at meeting. Will have to come back.
Comments: Owner was present with necessary paperwork; Fee paid $368.40; Permit
per Roi’s questions. issued. He will check height of house and set-backs
A. 146 Antler given extension for removal of chain link Issues/Concerns/Complaints:
fence. Certified letters from lawyer will go out for 158 Antler, fence violation. Vacant Lot 108, dead
B. Public Comments: Marilyn Wood had questions about adding a closet, Bryan
directed her to get Joint Permit Request from Alto CC office. Bruce Ripko (homeowner)
with Manny (contractor) wanted to present a permit request, which was not on the agenda,
they were directed to submit request to Maggie by July 10, 20 in order to be included on the
9. Informal Discussions: Bryan will be gone till September (return
tbd); Cindi will begin to take on the Agenda and Minutes and assist
C. Invoice Approvals No invoices for 7/2/20
Next Regular meeting: 16 July 2020 at 9:00; Stag Room of ALG&CC; 1 Mulligan Dr,
Alto, NM. Submission deadline for permit applications is 3:00pm 10 July 2020
The agenda for the meeting can be revised and published up to seventy-two hours prior to the meeting and will be
posted on the public bulletin board at ALG&CC, Alto, NM. At any time during the Open Meeting the Commission
may close the meeting to the public to discuss matters not subject to the New Mexico Open Meetings Act. The
Commission may revise the order of Agenda items considered at this Open Meeting.
